learning_outcomes.description: Understand how communication impacts management and leadership in a range of organisations.
Gain knowledge about the role of communication in teamwork, negotiation in the workplace, and conflict resolution.
Identify how interpersonal dynamics, gender and culture impact communication in local, national, and global contexts.
Develop the skills to showcase yourself as a potential manager and leader in the communication management domain.
Ability to demonstrate and deliver effective verbal and nonverbal communication skills in the workplace context.

assessments.due_date:
Week 2 Mon - Week 7 Mon

In-class activity in Weeks 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 & 7
assessments.task_description:
In-class quizzes comprising of multiple choice and/or true or false questions

This is an in-class only activity for which you must be present. Quizzes in total will be worth 40 marks, and each question is worth 1 mark, and thus, there will be a total of 40 questions over 6 quizzes, worth 40% of the course grade.

The quiz will be made available in-class only at the end of every lecture. You will have a 10-minute window to complete the quiz, in an invigilated environment.

These are closed book quizzes with no other study materials permitted.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This is an in-class only activity for which you must be present. The quizzes will be administered in class. These are timed quizzes, to be completed within 10 minutes from start to finish.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission is not possible for this piece of assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
Week 9 - Week 13

In-person presentation in class in Weeks 9, 11, 12 & 13.
assessments.task_description:
You are required to make a 3-minute elevator pitch presentation as if you are taking part in a job interview (worth 10%). Following your pitch, you will be asked one question by the interview team on the spot based on your pitch (worth 10%).

Presentations will happen alphabetically (as per enrollment list from SI-Net) only during class contact times in Weeks 9, 11, 12 & 13.

Please note that there may be some variation to the number of weeks required for this in-class task depending on student enrollment numbers. If any change is necessitated, the same will be communicated to you by the course coordinator well in advance.

The purpose of the presentation is to provide a practice arena for how to communicate and present yourself putting your 'best foot forward' to make the best impression as you graduate and (re)enter the job market.

On the day of your pitch (in-class), you must deliver it in person without technical aids or reading from any written script. Pitch presentations will be recorded for marking purposes and associated records.

Numerous learning resources are available on the course BB site to assist with this assessment task. More task details will follow in Blackboard.

Recording of Oral and Practical Assessment

  • All presentations will be recorded for marking purposes via recording facilities available where the assessment takes place (eg. ECHO360, Zoom, and/or camera device).
  • Recordings will be retained by the School of Communication and Arts for at least 12 months from the release of the final grade for the course.
  • Recordings will be stored in a secure manner and will only be accessed by authorised school staff for the purposes of:
  • Moderation of marking;
  • Provision of feedback to the student(s) recorded; and/or
  • Re-marking following a successful re-mark application.
assessments.submission_guidelines: On the day of your pitch (in-class), you will deliver it in person, without technical aids or reading from any written script. You must pitch for specific positions given by the course coordinator. If you pitch for any other position, you will automatically be awarded a zero. Pitching for the correct position is a hurdle which you must pass to be eligible to be graded for this assessment task.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission is not possible for this piece of assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
The purpose of the End-of-Semester Examination (delivered via Inspera platform) is to test your knowledge and understanding of the issues covered in this course -- in lectures and specified reading material. Accordingly, the examination will cover topics taught in lectures and specified topics taken from any specified reading material.

The exam questions will test your comprehension of the major ideas and debates covered in the course, as well as your ability to think critically about them and to apply the skills and understanding you have developed to different examples or contexts. Further information will be provided on the Blackboard site for this course during the semester, and a practice exam opportunity will be available in class (announced in class).
assessments.submission_guidelines: UQ uses a secure assessment tool, Inspera for exam/assessment. Inspera Assessment provides additional functionality to complement the existing suite of digital learning tools at UQ with a greater range of question types and accessibility provisions.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 8: Inspera Exam and Interview Pitch Delivery & Practice

This week's seminar is focused on learning how to access and complete a mock exam in INSPERA to prepare you for the final exam (centrally timetabled). BRING YOUR DEVICES THIS WEEK SO SAFE EXAM BROWSER SOFTWARE CAN BE DOWNLOADED AND INSTALLED ON YOUR DEVICE IN PREPARATION FOR THE FINAL EXAM.

It will also focus on improving oral presentation skills in view of interview pitches (graded assessment task).

Learning outcomes: L04
